target.name: Einride
target.founder: Robert Falck, Filip Lilja, Linnéa Kornehed

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Einride, a Series C autonomous transportation company, was last valued at $1.8B post-money after raising $752M+ to date. The valuation landscape in Transportation remains sparse: while seven private peers operate in the sector, comparable public benchmarks are absent. The narrow peer set—including Rivian, Harbinger, and ZeroAvia—lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints to support a reliable peer-median triangulation.

The analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $1.8B last post-money valuation. This method yields an implied valuation of $3.1B. Given the limitations in peer comparability and the absence of public market anchors, a single-method triangulation carries elevated uncertainty. The range—$2.6B to $3.5B—reflects sensitivity around the step-up multiplier assumption rather than convergence across independent methods.

The primary constraint is insufficient homogeneous comps at Einride's stage and scale within Transportation. A more robust valuation would require either broader sector expansion to identify adjacent-stage peers or materialization of comparable public benchmarks. The median estimate of $3.1B is defensible given historical Series C progressions, but represents a modest 72% step from current valuation—a modest premium that warrants validation against actual market conditions and company-specific momentum indicators.
